# Radio — Designer Flexibility Test

> Gate 0 file — retroactively completed. All use-cases traced through
> radio.tokens.css and radio.css.

---

## Use-case 1: Full rebrand

A product switches its brand color from blue to indigo. No component CSS changes.

```css
--color-brand-500: hsl(240, 70%, 55%);
/* + all other brand steps regenerated by palette engine */
```

Chain trace:
- `--color-brand-500` → `--radio-bg-checked` (declared as `var(--color-brand-500)`)
- `--radio-bg-checked` → `--_r-bg` (set by `:checked` selector)
- `--_r-bg` → `.radio__circle { background-color }` in checked state
- Dot (filled variant): `--radio-dot-color` → `--color-fixed-white` — stays white ✅
- Focus ring: `--focus-ring-color` → brand-500 → automatically matches

✅ — no gaps. Rebrand propagates through the color token chain automatically.

---

## Use-case 2: Global shape reset (all components become sharp)

Radio buttons are always circular by design — shape reset does NOT apply to radio circles. The circle `border-radius` is hardcoded to `9999px` (not driven by `--radius-*` tokens) because a non-circular radio button breaks the dot-in-ring visual metaphor.

```css
/* Shape reset has no effect on radio circles — intentional */
--radius-full: 0;  /* → does NOT change radio circles */
```

Chain trace:
- `--radio-circle-size-{size}` → `.radio__circle { width, height }` 
- `border-radius: 9999px` — hardcoded, does NOT use `--radius-full`

✅ — shape reset correctly has no effect on radio. The circular shape is a hard contract, not a customization point. Designers who want a square radio button must create a custom variant explicitly.

---

## Use-case 3: Density override (make base feel larger)

A product wants the `base` size to feel like `medium`.

```css
--radio-circle-size-base: var(--radio-circle-size-medium);
--radio-dot-size-base:    var(--radio-dot-size-medium);
--radio-label-font-size-base: var(--radio-label-font-size-medium);
--radio-gap-base:         var(--radio-gap-medium);
```

Chain trace:
- `--radio-circle-size-base` → `--_r-circle-size` (set by `[data-size="base"]`)
- `--_r-circle-size` → `.radio__circle { width, height }`
- `--radio-dot-size-base` → `--_r-dot-size` → `.radio__dot { width, height }`
- Dot is centered via `margin: auto` in flexbox — stays centered at new size ✅

✅ — all four properties (circle, dot, font, gap) recompute. No hardcoded pixel values in the selector rules.

---

## Use-case 4: Dark mode (automatic, no extra rules)

System switches to `[data-theme="dark"]` on root.

Chain trace:
- `[data-theme="dark"]` → T1 overrides `--color-brand-500`, `--color-neutral-300`, `--color-neutral-400`, etc.
- `--radio-bg-checked` → `--color-brand-500` (updated) → checked ring fill updates
- `--radio-border-color` → `--color-neutral-300` → dark mode maps to a lighter neutral step → unchecked border updates
- Dot (filled): `--radio-dot-color` → `--color-fixed-white` → stays white in dark mode ✅

✅ — radio needs NO component-level `[data-theme="dark"]` block. T1 color system handles all semantic tokens.

---

## Use-case 5: Custom role ("success" radio for confirmation UI)

A product wants radio buttons in a confirmed/selected step to use green.

```css
.my-success-radio {
  --radio-bg-checked:           var(--color-success-500);
  --radio-border-color-checked: var(--color-success-500);
  --radio-bg-checked-hover:     var(--color-success-600);
  --radio-bg-checked-pressed:   var(--color-success-700);
}
```

Chain trace:
- `--radio-bg-checked` overridden → `--_r-bg` picks it up when `:checked` → green fill ✅
- Hover/pressed: explicit overrides needed (same limitation as toggle and checkbox)

⚠️ — same documented limitation: hover/pressed shades require explicit overrides for a full custom-role rebrand. This is consistent with the toggle and checkbox limitation — all three form controls share this pattern.

---

## Use-case 6: Horizontal radio group (layout direction)

A product needs options displayed side-by-side instead of stacked.

```html
<fieldset class="radio-group" data-direction="horizontal">
  ...
</fieldset>
```

```css
/* No CSS override needed — data-direction="horizontal" is handled by component CSS */
.radio-group[data-direction="horizontal"] {
  flex-direction: row;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}
```

Chain trace:
- `data-direction="horizontal"` → CSS selector `.radio-group[data-direction="horizontal"]` activates
- `flex-direction: row` + `gap: var(--radio-group-gap-{size})` → horizontal layout
- Each `.radio` label maintains its own min-height tap target ✅
- Responsive: `flex-wrap: wrap` allows items to wrap on narrow containers ✅

✅ — no additional CSS overrides needed. The group layout direction is a data attribute, not a CSS class, consistent with other DTF variant patterns.

---

## ✅ Flexibility review checklist (must all be checked before Gate 0 passes)

- [x] Rebrand use-case fully traced — no gaps
- [x] Shape reset use-case: circle is intentionally NOT shape-resettable — documented as hard contract
- [x] Density override use-case fully traced — no hardcoded values found
- [x] Dark mode use-case fully traced — confirms NO component-level dark block needed
- [x] Custom role use-case fully traced — 4-token override required for checked+hover+pressed; documented limitation
- [x] Component-specific use-case added (horizontal group layout) and traced — no gaps
- [x] Any gaps found: none blocking; hover/pressed custom role limitation documented (consistent with checkbox and toggle)
